notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms in 2.39 are sāṅkhye / yoge (the two named methods), buddhi (wisdom / intellect), abhihitā / śṛṇu (declared / hear — the meta-act of teaching), buddhyā yuktaḥ (joined to wisdom), and karma-bandham prahāsyasi (you will cast off the bondage of action). The engineering layer preserves all five and — critically — preserves the META function of the verse: it does not flatten 2.39 into 'two methods, here they are'; it explicitly names the verse as the one in which Krishna stops arguing a position and instead names the architecture of his own teaching ('Krishna stops arguing a position and instead names the architecture of his own teaching'). The three commentarial readings — Shankara (parallel disciplines, jñāna and karma-yoga), Ramanuja (sequential moments — sāṅkhya as precondition, yoga as the disposition for acting in its light), Vishvanatha (yoga as bhakti-yoga) — are all presented in the source pack with named traditions. The engineering layer adopts Shankara's reading explicitly (parallel disciplines, both required, neither reducible) and is honest in the counter-example that the Vishishtadvaita and Gaudiya readings are not preserved at the same level of structural fidelity. This is exactly the kind of disclosure the source-fidelity gate is looking for. PASS.
